syntheticDeclaration.purpose: SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURE. Documents the alert-lifecycle (override/downtime/handoff/recovery) dangerous-miss family so it is tracked as a first-class hazard even though no stateful workflow engine exists yet in this repository. No patient or workflow-event data is described.

executionBinding.engineRationale: assessPediatricAnemia is a stateless pure function (src/engine.js): it has no alert queue, no override/acknowledge affordance, no downtime detector, no handoff record, and no recovery/reconciliation path (grep for 'override'/'downtime'/'handoff'/'recovery' across src/, modules/, scripts/ on 2026-07-19 returns no such implementation, only unrelated CSS-override and reference-interval-override matches). Building alert lifecycle state requires the owner-held V4/V5 protocol-through-adjudication contracts this task must not invent.
executionBinding.blockedOnTask: arc-clinical-council-adoption-v1 P4-T4 (V4 silent-mode / V5 alert-lifecycle, override, downtime, handoff, recovery contracts) — see plan §4 P4-T4 and pediatric_clinical_review.json seat PCR-SAFETY finding PEDS-HF-001

expectedBehavior.requiredSignal: Recoverable audit trail and no silent completion state.
forbiddenSignals: any test or code path in this repository reporting this hazard family as covered, passed, or mitigated absent a real alert-lifecycle engine

treating the rule-evaluation audit trail (provenance.ruleAudit) as if it satisfied override/downtime/handoff/recovery coverage — it proves only that no rule silently drops from a single stateless evaluation, not that a multi-step workflow state is recoverable

expectedTrace.note: No executable engine exists for this hazard family as of 2026-07-19. The one adjacent, already-executable property is assessPediatricAnemia's per-run completeness proof (provenance.evaluatedRuleCount equals modules/anemia/rules.json's rule count, and every rule appears in provenance.ruleAudit) — asserted separately in the test lane as a partial, explicitly-labeled signal, not as coverage of this hazard.

rollbackSignal: Re-author this scenario with a real executionBinding.engine and expectedBehavior once an alert-lifecycle (override/downtime/handoff/recovery) engine and owner-approved V4/V5 protocol exist (tracked under P4-T4).
